conceptualization.theme_description: Color detailed coral reefs bustling with marine life.
conceptualization.painting_approach: For this abstract watercolor painting, we will avoid defining the boundaries with hard outlines. Instead, we'll use fluid, merging colors to mimic the watery, constantly changing environment of ocean reefs.

techniques.name: Wet-on-wet
techniques.description: Apply wet paint onto wet paper to allow for color bleeding, creating the illusion of underwater blurring and the soft edges of marine flora.

techniques.name: Salt texturing
techniques.description: Sprinkle salt on wet paint areas to achieve a textured pattern that mirrors the grainy ocean floor and reefs.

techniques.name: Dabbing
techniques.description: Dab lightly with a sponge for the illusion of coral and porous rock textures.

techniques.name: Layering
techniques.description: Layer lighter tones over darker ones to bring out the depth and detail in marine life forms.

composition.focal_points: A cluster of coral at the center, surrounded by hints of marine life like shadows of fish and reef creatures.
composition.flow: Lead the viewer’s eye through the painting using the flow of water-like color strokes that hint at current movement.

conceptualization.emotive_goals: The abstract style should evoke a sense of wonder and the impermanence of nature, highlighting the fragile beauty of oceanic reefs.
